userStories.title: Add heartbeat history, stale detection, notifications, and archived component visibility
acceptanceCriteria: Checkybot stores time-series history for component heartbeats and statuses and exposes that history in the Application UI.
Components that stop reporting are automatically marked stale and escalated to danger based on their declared interval, with automated coverage of the timeout behavior.
Warning, danger, and stale events flow through existing Checkybot notification rules, and pruned components retain archived history that remains viewable in the UI.

userStories.title: Add package health components, heartbeats, and current application status
acceptanceCriteria: Developers can define named health components in the Laravel package with numeric or boolean metrics and warning/danger thresholds.
The scheduled package command evaluates due components locally and sends raw metrics plus computed status to Checkybot.
Checkybot shows current component status and overall worst-component-wins Application status, with end-to-end tests covering package definition, heartbeat ingest, and UI-visible status.

userStories.title: Sync external package checks to Applications from the scheduled command
acceptanceCriteria: One scheduled package command can register the Application if needed and then sync external checks from `routes/checkybot.php` onto that Application.
Synced uptime, SSL, and API checks are visible as package-managed checks on the Application record in Checkybot.
Removing a package-managed external check from code removes it from the active Application config while preserving archived history, with end-to-end tests for sync and pruning.

userStories.title: Package API-key auth and application registration handshake
acceptanceCriteria: Package traffic authenticates with the documented Bearer API-key flow rather than relying on the current inconsistent runtime path.
A package install with guided setup data attaches to the intended existing Application shell on first registration.
A package install without a pre-created shell auto-creates a new Application when no matching `identity_endpoint + environment` exists, with tests covering both flows.

userStories.title: Applications foundation and guided setup snippet
acceptanceCriteria: An operator can create an Application in Checkybot with required fields `name` and `environment` only.
Checkybot shows a guided setup view for the Application with a full copy-paste Laravel install snippet, including hidden application ID pairing data.
Automated tests cover Application creation and install snippet generation from the user-facing flow.
